conversation.content: You are a customer service agent for a travel company. Today's date: <REDACTED>. You are currently helping customer user_002. Use the available tools to help the customer.
Hi, I need to move my flight BK-1000 from June 25 to June 18 instead — something came up and I need to fly earlier. My user ID is user_002.

I found your current booking and checked June 18 options from ORD to SFO.

Available replacement flight:
- UA101
- Departs: Jun 18 at 1:30 PM
- Arrives: 6:14 PM
- Nonstop
- Economy fare currently: $380

Your current booking:
- BK-1000
- UA100 on Jun 25 at 11:45 AM
- Economy
- Window seat
- Vegetarian meal
- No Wi‑Fi, extra legroom, or insurance

I can change you to UA101 and keep the same preferences. Since this is a flight change to a different flight, I’ll need to submit it as:
- cabin: economy
- seat: window
- meal: vegetarian
- Wi‑Fi: no
- extra legroom: no
- insurance: no

If you want, I can go ahead and process the change now.

result.message: No flights found matching your criteria.

conversation.content: Before I approve it, what actually determines the change fee here? I was assuming the new flight timing might drive it, but I want to understand what sets the fee first.

The fee is not driven by the time of day of the new flight.

For your ticket, the change cost is determined by:
- fare class: economy
- route type: domestic
- timing relative to your original departure date
- reason for the change
- plus any fare difference

For your case, the policy says:
- Domestic economy change fee:
  - $75 if the original departure is more than 7 days away
  - $150 if the original departure is 7 days away or less
- Fare difference also applies

Important nuance:
- The “timing” that matters is how close you are to departure when the change is made, not whether the new flight is morning vs afternoon.
- Special reasons can reduce or waive the fee:
  - medical: 50% off
  - bereavement: 75% off
  - jury duty / military / airline schedule change / weather: no change fee

For BK-1000:
- Original flight departs Jun 25
- Today is Jun 15
- That is 10 days before departure
- So if this is a personal change, the standard change fee would be $75
- Your current fare was $350
- The new flight’s economy fare is $380
- So the fare difference appears to be $30

Estimated total if processed as a personal change:
- $75 change fee
- $30 fare difference
- Estimated total: $105

If you want, I can process it as:
- personal
or, if one of the exception reasons applies, tell me which one and I’ll use that instead.

rules.free_change_window: Free changes within 24 hours (domestic) or 48 hours (international) of booking
rules.economy_domestic_personal: Domestic change fee: $75 if departure >7 days, $150 if ≤7 days. Fare difference also applies.
rules.economy_international_personal: International change fee: $100 if departure >7 days, $200 if ≤7 days. Fare difference also applies.
rules.economy_medical: Medical changes: 50% discount on standard fee (requires change_reason='medical')
rules.economy_bereavement: Bereavement changes: 75% discount on standard fee (requires change_reason='bereavement')
rules.schedule_change: Airline schedule changes: free, no fee (change_reason='schedule_change')
rules.route_type_matters: Fees differ by route type. Check flight route_type.
